ux_issues.description: Chat session mode field shows CHAT_MODE_COMPANION even though the first message was sent in CHAT_MODE_SELECTION. The session mode reflects the last mode used rather than tracking mode transitions.
ux_issues.suggestion: Consider preserving the mode of the first message in the session, or tracking mode history per session.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: AI created a Day 3 itinerary with a 4-5 hour Sintra day trip, but Jake explicitly stated he has a 4pm airport departure on his last day. A Sintra day trip is unrealistic given a 10pm arrival (Day 1) and 4pm airport departure (Day 3). The AI did not account for the compressed timeline.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Selection mode initial response. AI created 10 itinerary items across 3 days including 'Day trip to Sintra (4-5 hours)' on the departure day. Jake said he has 2 days and lands at 10pm with a return flight departure requiring 4pm airport arrival.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Initial selection-mode response was building into a lengthy text reply (trip essay format) on top of the 10 itinerary items already created via tool. For a user at the airport gate, the AI should have kept the text response short and pointed to the itinerary items instead of writing a narrative summary.
ai_behavior_issues.context: First message in selection mode. The AI created the trip and 10 itinerary items (good), but then started writing a long text summary. The stream was truncated at 388 chars visible but the full response appeared to be heading toward a multi-paragraph format.

ai_behavior_issues.issue: Companion mode photography response (2556 chars) was longer than ideal for a traveler on-the-go. While the photographer context justified more detail, companion mode responses should aim for brevity. The late-night arrival (871 chars) and airport logistics (851 chars) responses were appropriately concise.
ai_behavior_issues.context: Second companion message asking about one-day must-dos as a photographer. The AI provided excellent content but was verbose compared to the other companion responses.

tool_failures.input: {"query": "best things to do in Lisbon 2 days 2025 2026 top highlights"}
tool_failures.error: Real-time web search is not configured in this environment. This is expected in local test environments.

usefulness_evaluation.narrative: The AI excelled at the urgent trip creation scenario -- it created the trip immediately without asking unnecessary clarifying questions, which is exactly what a last-minute traveler needs. Companion mode responses for late-night arrival guidance and airport logistics were concise and actionable with specific names, neighborhoods, and transit details. The expert handoff to a Portugal photography specialist was smooth and added value. The main weakness was the itinerary quality: the AI created a 3-day plan including a Sintra day trip on departure day, not accounting for the 10pm arrival and 4pm departure constraint that makes this effectively a 1.5-day trip. Despite this, the Lisbon-specific knowledge was strong (late dining culture, Tram 28, Pasteis de Belem, metro to airport, Alfama viewpoints).
